title: Layer 3: Separate orchestrator session from CEO session
description: Architect and implement the split of pipeline orchestration from the CEO session. CEO session only touches approve + completion gates. Dedicated orchestrator handles triage through execute autonomously.

in: Create orchestrator agent and entry point
Refactor SKILL.md into thin CEO dispatcher
Gate handoff protocol in pipeline docs

dod.criterion: CEO session spawns CLI orchestrator instead of running 14 steps inline
Orchestrator halts at approve and completion gates, CEO resumes and decides
Post-gate resume spawns new orchestrator to continue from next step
End-to-end walkthrough validates both lightweight and heavyweight directive flows

tasks.title: Design the orchestrator-CEO session split architecture
tasks.user_scenario: CEO invokes /directive and a CLI orchestrator runs autonomously, pausing only at approve and completion gates.
tasks.scope: Produce architecture design doc: session boundary, handoff protocol, directive.json as shared state, entry point changes. Address all platform constraints.

dod.criterion: Design doc exists at projects/orchestrator-split/architecture-design.md
Doc specifies which steps run in orchestrator vs CEO, with rationale
Doc defines gate protocol: how orchestrator signals, how CEO resumes, using directive.json fields
Doc addresses all 4 platform constraints with explicit mitigations
Doc includes step-by-step lifecycle flow for heavyweight directive across both sessions

tasks.title: Create orchestrator agent and entry point
tasks.user_scenario: A .claude/agents/orchestrator.md agent exists that can be CLI-spawned to run pipeline steps autonomously.
tasks.scope: Create orchestrator agent definition. Register in agent-registry.json. Create or modify SKILL entry point variant.

dod.criterion: .claude/agents/orchestrator.md exists with personality, permissions, and pipeline execution instructions
agent-registry.json includes orchestrator with distinct id, title, role
Agent references pipeline docs and specifies step execution loop
STOP gates defined: approve (heavyweight) and completion (all weights)

tasks.title: Refactor SKILL.md into thin CEO dispatcher
tasks.user_scenario: CEO invokes /directive and SKILL.md spawns orchestrator and returns immediately instead of running all steps.
tasks.scope: Refactor SKILL.md: triage + spawn + gate handling. Handle resume case (detect approve/completion in directive.json). Move step execution content to orchestrator-facing doc.

dod.criterion: SKILL.md is under 80 lines of active instruction, focused on triage + spawn + gate handling
CEO session spawns orchestrator via spawn-agent.ts with directive context
SKILL.md handles resume: detects approve/completion in directive.json and runs gate inline
Pipeline steps table and execution loop relocated to orchestrator-facing doc

tasks.title: Implement gate handoff protocol in pipeline docs
tasks.user_scenario: Orchestrator writes specific status to directive.json at gates and halts. CEO can resume without re-running the pipeline.
tasks.scope: Update 07-plan-approval.md and 11-completion-gate.md with orchestrator halt behavior. Add awaiting_ceo convention. Update directive-json.md and 00-delegation-and-triage.md.

dod.criterion: 07-plan-approval.md has orchestrator mode section with halt behavior
11-completion-gate.md has equivalent orchestrator halt behavior
directive-json.md includes any new status values for gate protocol
00-delegation-and-triage.md no longer assumes current session runs all steps

tasks.title: Implement orchestrator resume after CEO gate decisions
tasks.user_scenario: After CEO approves, a new orchestrator spawns to continue from the next step.
tasks.scope: Implement continuation: CEO approve → spawn new orchestrator from project-brainstorm. Completion reopen → spawn orchestrator from plan. Support resume mode in orchestrator.

dod.criterion: After approve gate, new orchestrator spawns continuing from project-brainstorm
After completion reopen, new orchestrator spawns from plan step
Orchestrator supports resume: reads directive.json, skips completed steps
Pre-flight cleanup doesn't kill orchestrator's own parent processes

tasks.title: End-to-end walkthrough test of split pipeline
tasks.user_scenario: A directive runs end-to-end through the split architecture: CEO triages, orchestrator runs, halts at gates, CEO resumes.
tasks.scope: Create test walkthrough and dry-run for both lightweight and heavyweight directive flows. Verify state transitions, clean exits, and resume behavior.

dod.criterion: Walkthrough report documents tested flows for lightweight and heavyweight paths
directive.json state transitions correct at each handoff point
Orchestrator exits cleanly at gates (no orphaned processes)
Resume flow works: CEO gate decision triggers correct continuation
